changedFiles.reason: Archived collaborative environment positioning Work Item Contract.
Archived collaborative environment positioning AI Change Summary.
Expanded cockpit positioning from a small governance layer to a collaborative engineering environment with governance as the core mechanism.
Aligned the Japanese cockpit workflow guide with the collaborative environment positioning and collaboration-oriented agent rules.
Generated cockpit status for the active Work Item lifecycle.
Updated generic agent rules to describe collaborative environment expectations, valid unknown outputs, summary handoff, and checkpoint purpose.
Aligned Claude-labeled agent rules with the shared collaborative environment model without introducing Claude-only semantics.
Aligned Gemini-labeled agent rules with the shared collaborative environment model.
Repositioned AI Cockpit as a collaborative engineering environment while preserving AI Change Governance as the control layer.
Aligned the Chinese overview with the new top-level positioning while preserving the governance core.
Aligned the Japanese overview with the new top-level positioning while preserving the governance core.
Clarified that the architecture supports collaborative human-agent work with governance as the control center.
Added collaborative environment design and 4D operating model sections mapped to existing mechanisms.
Updated launch positioning to match the collaborative environment framing without dropping governance language.
Updated distributed template rules to describe the collaborative environment model consistently across agents.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Keep AI Change Governance as the core enforcement mechanism; add collaborative environment design above it rather than replacing it.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: README, cockpit docs, architecture, and launch copy now describe collaborative environment design while explicitly retaining AI Change Governance as the control layer.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Treat Delegation, Description, Discernment, and Diligence as an explanatory operating model only; do not introduce them as required runtime schema fields.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: 4D language appears only in documentation narrative and does not modify Contract schema, scripts, checks, or guards.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Do not make the repository sound Claude-only or tied to any single agent vendor; wording must apply equally to Codex, Claude, Gemini, Cursor, Antigravity, and similar agents.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: README, cockpit docs, and agent rule files explicitly preserve multi-agent language and align Claude/Gemini files to the shared model.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Preserve the existing bounded, reviewable, and auditable framing.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: The core bounded, reviewable, and auditable phrasing remains in the main README and is reinforced by governance-oriented documentation.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Do not change scripts, guard policies, checks, CI, or tests in this Work Item.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: All edits are limited to documentation, agent-rule markdown, Work Item records, and generated status.

risk.detail: Residual risk is limited to wording interpretation across English, Chinese, and Japanese documentation rather than runtime behavior.

residualRisks.detail: Chinese and Japanese phrasing is directionally aligned with the English positioning but should still be reviewed by native readers for tone nuance.

reviewReadiness.reason: The change is documentation-only and review should focus on positioning consistency and preservation of the governance core.
expectedReviewFocus: Confirm that collaborative environment design is presented as the higher-level framing and AI Change Governance remains the core mechanism.
Confirm that 4D is documented only as an explanatory model, not as a new schema or enforcement requirement.
Confirm that agent rules remain vendor-neutral and treat unknowns, summaries, and checkpoints as collaboration support.

checkpointEvidence.evidence: Ran make check-ai-contract and make ai-checkpoint after updating the Contract to the final documentation scope.
Ran make ai-checkpoint after completing the documentation and agent-rule updates to confirm scope, acceptance, and review focus before finish.

overclaimPrevention: Do not report completion for checks or behavior that were not verified.
